question.text: A call option gives the buyer the right to:

answer.text: Buy the underlying asset at the strike price on or before the expiry date
Sell the underlying asset at the strike price on or before the expiry date
Both buy and sell the underlying asset at the strike price
Receive dividends from the underlying asset until the expiry date

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** A CALL option gives the buyer the RIGHT TO BUY the underlying asset at the predetermined strike price. The word "call" can be remembered as "calling the asset to you" (buying).
**The Trap:** A PUT option gives the right to SELL, not a call. Students often confuse call (buy) and put (sell).
**Cross-Exam Context:** This is the most basic options concept tested across all NISM derivative exams.
**Key Takeaway:** Call = Right to BUY. Put = Right to SELL.
